name: Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop
name_original: Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro – paseo circular
description: Explore the historic 13th-century Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro on this moderate 3.4-mile circular hike with panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Burguillos Del Cerro offers varied terrain, from the Sierra Caliza (Limestone Mountain Range) to dehesas and forests of oak and cork oak. The region is characterized by its natural beauty and historical landmarks, including the Sierra del Cordel, known for its geological features. Hikers can explore routes that pass through picturesque landscapes and lead to significant sites like the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro. The area also features the tranquil Pantano de Valuengo, providing opportunities for birdwatching. Best hiking trails around Burguillos Del Cerro The most popular hiking route is Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views from the historic 13th-century castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194), a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail explores the characteristic landscapes of the Sierra Caliza. Local hikers also love the Ride to Trasierra - circular from La Parra, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through dehesas and forests, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Burguillos Del Cerro is defined by limestone mountain ranges, oak and cork oak forests, and historical sites like the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Burguillos Del Cerro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 190 hikers have used komoot to explore Burguillos Del Cerro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Burguillos Del Cerro?
A: Burguillos Del Cerro offers a diverse network of over 50 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, including easy walks, moderate excursions, and a few more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Burguillos Del Cerro?
A: The trails in Burguillos Del Cerro range from easy to difficult. You'll find around 16 easy trails, typically lasting up to 2 hours with minimal elevation gain, suitable for all fitness levels. There are also about 34 moderate hikes, which can extend up to 5 hours and involve more significant elevation changes, requiring good physical condition. For those seeking a challenge, there are a couple of difficult routes that demand very good physical fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Burguillos Del Cerro?
A: Hikers in Burguillos Del Cerro can expect to traverse diverse landscapes characteristic of Extremadura. These include the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Caliza (Limestone Mountain Range)&lt;/strong&gt;, picturesque &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and forests dominated by oak and cork oak trees. The region also features the geologically rich &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Cordel&lt;/strong&gt;, home to the &lt;strong&gt;Minas Monchi&lt;/strong&gt;, a Site of Scientific Interest.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Burguillos Del Cerro has approximately 16 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes are generally shorter, with less elevation gain, making them accessible for a relaxed outdoor experience. For example, the historic town center offers urban routes through its &lt;strong&gt;Historic-Artistic Complex&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring steep streets, historic buildings, and fountains.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Burguillos Del Cerro are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991603066/limestone-mountain-range-circular-from-la-parra-sl-ba-194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone Mountain Range – circular from La Parra (SL-BA 194)&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the characteristic Sierra Caliza. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991605926/ride-to-trasierra-circular-from-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to Trasierra - circular from La Parra&lt;/a&gt;, leading through beautiful dehesas and forests.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I visit on a hike?
A: Many hiking routes in Burguillos Del Cerro incorporate historical landmarks. The most prominent is the &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro&lt;/strong&gt;, a 13th-century Moorish castle later occupied by the Knights Templar. Access to the castle is often on foot, offering panoramic 360-degree views. You can explore this on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991273131/burguillos-del-cerro-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The town's old quarter, a &lt;strong&gt;Historic-Artistic Complex&lt;/strong&gt;, also provides urban routes to sites like the Church of la Misericordia y Judería and the Parish of Santa María de la Encina y San Juan Bautista.

Q: Is Burguillos Del Cerro a good destination for birdwatching?
A: Yes, the region is excellent for birdwatching, particularly around the &lt;strong&gt;Pantano de Valuengo (Valuengo Reservoir)&lt;/strong&gt;. Located a short distance from Burguillos del Cerro, this natural treasure offers tranquility and beautiful scenery, making it an ideal spot to observe local birdlife.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Burguillos Del Cerro?
A: The hiking trails around Burguillos Del Cerro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks, and the panoramic views, especially from the castle.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991273131/burguillos-del-cerro-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for its stunning panoramic views from the historic 13th-century castle, perched on a ridge overlooking the surrounding area. This moderate route provides exceptional vistas of the Extremaduran landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Burguillos Del Cerro?
A: While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter hikes can also be rewarding, though some routes might require more preparation. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that start from nearby towns?
A: Yes, some trails originate from neighboring towns, expanding your hiking options. For example, you can find routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande, Zafra – Plaza Chica loop from Zafra&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Los Santos de Maimona – Vía de la Plata Sign loop from Zafra&lt;/a&gt;, both starting from Zafra.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for dogs?
A: Many of the natural trails through the dehesas and forests are generally suitable for dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations regarding pets in natural areas. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you carry water for them, especially on longer routes.
tips: On the top of the cliff that dominates the town of Burguillos del Cerro is the Castle of Muslim origin and of a defensive nature. It was built in the 13th century and was later passed into the hands of the Order of the Temple.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro circular walk?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail is centered around the village of Burguillos del Cerro. You'll likely find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, from where you can access the path leading up to the castle.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro circular walk dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is generally suitable for dogs, but be mindful of the terrain, especially near the castle. The path leading to the castle includes a cobblestone road and stairs. Within the castle ruins, there are uneven surfaces and crumbling stairs, which might be challenging for some pets. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of cattle farms in the broader area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the historical nature and potential for sun exposure, spring and autumn are likely the most pleasant times to visit. These seasons offer milder temperatures for hiking and exploring the castle. Summer can be very hot in this region of Spain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the castle or trail?
faqs.answer: No, the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro is freely accessible 24/7. You do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to explore the castle or hike the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the circular walk?
faqs.answer: The path leading to the castle includes a paved section, a cobblestone road, and some stairs near the entrance. Within the castle itself, expect uneven surfaces, crumbling stairs, and low walls. The broader area around Burguillos del Cerro features olive groves and cattle farms, suggesting varied natural paths.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the historic Burguillos del Cerro Castle, a 13th-century fortress built over Moorish foundations. From the castle, especially the keep, you'll get panoramic views of the village and surrounding landscape. Keep an eye out for kestrels near the battlements.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Burguillos del Cerro circular walk suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The hike to the castle is considered moderate due to the ascent. While the circular walk itself is not overly long (around 5.5 km), the castle ruins present challenges with uneven surfaces, crumbling stairs, and low walls. It's not considered a health and safety compliant site, so families with young children or beginners should exercise caution and be prepared for these conditions.

faqs.question: What should I know about safety when exploring the castle?
faqs.answer: The castle is not considered a health and safety compliant site. Be aware of uneven surfaces, crumbling stairs, and low walls, particularly on the wall walk where crenels may be missing. Access is unattended, so visitors should navigate carefully and be prepared for the possibility of being the sole visitor. Always watch your step.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the village of Burguillos del Cerro. While specific amenities on the trail are not mentioned, you can expect to find cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation options within the village itself.

faqs.question: Are there any unique features or wildlife to look out for?
faqs.answer: Beyond the historical significance of the castle, a unique aspect is the opportunity to observe kestrels near the battlements. The castle's free and unattended access also offers a rare chance to explore a historic site at your own pace, often in solitude.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Wear sturdy hiking shoes suitable for varied terrain, including cobblestones and uneven surfaces within the castle. Layers are advisable, especially during spring and autumn. Bring water, snacks, and a camera to capture the panoramic views from the castle.
